title-group.article-title: Semantic Document Parsing Using RDF Knowledge Graphs

contrib.aff: Department of Computer Science, Example University

abstract.p: This paper presents a novel approach to document parsing using RDF knowledge graphs. We demonstrate how structured semantic representations enable more powerful querying and analysis of document collections.

sec.title: Introduction
sec.p: Document parsing systems traditionally convert documents to proprietary data structures. We propose a different approach based on W3C standards.
Our system, RuddyDoc, parses documents into an RDF knowledge graph stored in an embedded Oxigraph datastore.

sec.title: Methods
sec.p: We implemented backend parsers for multiple document formats including PDF, DOCX, HTML, Markdown, and LaTeX.

sec.title: Ontology Design
sec.p: The document ontology defines classes such as Paragraph, SectionHeader, TableElement, and PictureElement.

sec.title: Test Data
sec.p: We evaluated the system on a corpus of 1,000 scientific papers. Table 1 shows the results.

sec.title: Results
sec.p: The RDF-based approach enables complex SPARQL queries across document collections. Performance improved by 10x compared to traditional approaches.

caption.p: Query performance comparison showing RuddyDoc versus baseline systems.

sec.title: Discussion
sec.p: Our results demonstrate that RDF-based document representations offer significant advantages for semantic search and analysis.
Future work will explore integration with machine learning models for layout analysis and table structure recognition.
